china_exposure_best_public_estimate: About 42% of China's 2025 crude imports from GAC-reported Saudi/Iraq/UAE/Oman/Kuwait/Qatar, plus Kpler estimate of 1.38 mb/d Iranian crude mostly relabeled; CGEP summarizes total Hormuz crude transit exposure at 45-50% of China's crude imports.
observed_data: GAC-reported 2025 supplier shares summarized by CGEP; EIA 2025 CAB shows 2024 China crude source mix with Middle East 54% and Russia 20%; JODI April 2026 highlight reports China crude imports at 9.39 mb/d, down 2.4 mb/d.
adjustment_behavior: Near-term cushioning through stock buffer, delayed/import-timing changes, use of sanctioned barrels already on water or bonded in China, and possible Saudi/UAE bypass volumes.
inference_vs_observed: Observed exposure and import decline; supplier substitution beyond broad Russia/Iran/bypass channels is inferred without cargo-level China destination data.
notes: Treat Malaysia/Indonesia origin shifts cautiously because Iranian barrels may be relabeled.

china_exposure_best_public_estimate: China's large refining system can cut runs to reduce crude import demand and preserve domestic product supply.
observed_data: NBS reports crude processing fell 5.8% y/y in April 2026 to 54.65 Mt and 9.1% y/y in May 2026 to 53.72 Mt; IEA says feedstock-constrained Asian/Middle East runs were cut sharply in April.
adjustment_behavior: Run cuts are an observed adjustment channel; China can reduce crude intake without a one-for-one fall in domestic fuel availability if inventories and product export controls absorb the gap.
notes: This is cleaner evidence than claims of a government SPR release.

china_exposure_best_public_estimate: Large aggregate Chinese crude stocks reduce immediate supply-security risk, but public data do not separate government SPR, commercial/operational stocks, bonded Iranian barrels, and floating storage cleanly.
observed_data: CGEP/Kayrros cites 1.39 bn bbl of oil in China storage as of 2026-03-02; IEA April OMR says China added 40 mb of crude to tanks in March 2026; s49.3 evidence matrix records EIA/IEA evidence against a large confirmed government-SPR draw.
adjustment_behavior: Best public read is commercial/operational stock use plus stock-building/stock-shifting, not confirmed large government SPR release.
inference_vs_observed: Observed aggregate stocks; ownership and SPR draw magnitude are inferred/opaque.
notes: Do not write 'China released a ton of SPR' without a public ownership bridge.

china_exposure_best_public_estimate: China is a major refiner and product exporter, so product-export controls can reallocate domestic fuel without additional crude imports.
observed_data: s49.3 notes Reuters/TradingView reporting of May 2026 refined-oil exports down 23.6% y/y on export curbs; NBS May refinery runs were down 9.1% y/y.
adjustment_behavior: Restricting new gasoline/diesel export commitments preserves domestic supply and exports the shock to regional product markets.
inference_vs_observed: Observed via market reporting; policy intent inferred from reported curbs.
notes: Secondary market reporting; label Reuters/TradingView as such.

china_exposure_best_public_estimate: CGEP summarizes 31% of China's 2025 LNG imports from the Middle East, led by Qatar at 28%; Qatar/UAE LNG has no practical Hormuz bypass in project KMZ tables.
observed_data: CGEP using GAC reports Middle East LNG share; s49.4 notes China has gas-storage/peak-shaving capacity but no unified public daily inventory dashboard.
adjustment_behavior: Short-term adjustment is mainly demand reduction, fuel switching in power/industry, reluctance to bid for expensive spot LNG, and limited seasonal relief after heating season.
inference_vs_observed: Exposure observed; exact substitution volumes and demand destruction inferred.
notes: Russian pipeline expansion is not a near-term substitute before the Far Eastern route; Central Asia has bottlenecks per CGEP.

china_exposure_best_public_estimate: China-specific public destination data for Gulf LPG/NGL were not located in this pass; global exposure is material because IEA/KMZ rows put about 30% of seaborne LPG exports through Hormuz.
observed_data: IEA April OMR says deepest initial oil-demand cuts were in Middle East and Asia Pacific, mainly naphtha, LPG, and jet fuel; s49.5 flags petrochemicals as a strong mechanism but weak inventory-data story.
adjustment_behavior: Likely adjustment is feedstock substitution where possible, lower PDH/petrochemical operating rates, and reduced LPG/naphtha demand rather than clean supplier replacement.
inference_vs_observed: Global disruption observed; China-specific substitution is inferred and low confidence.
notes: Needs customs/tanker destination data for propane/butane/naphtha before making a China volume claim.

china_exposure_best_public_estimate: No clean China-specific Hormuz fertilizer import substitution estimate found; exposure is indirect through sulfur/ammonia/urea/MAP-DAP markets and domestic chemical/feedstock costs.
observed_data: KMZ/s49.5 tables record high global Hormuz exposure for sulfur, urea, ammonia, and MAP/DAP, but also note weak public stock/inventory data.
adjustment_behavior: For China, likely channels are price transmission and industrial input adjustment, not a clear emergency replacement-supplier story from public data.
inference_vs_observed: Mostly inferred for China; global exposure observed.
notes: Keep as a caveated side channel unless f6r.1 later finds China destination volumes.

china_exposure_best_public_estimate: China can absorb part of the shock through weaker oil demand, EV/LNG truck displacement of transport fuels, lower refinery runs, and gas/petrochemical demand response.
observed_data: EIA 2025 China CAB notes China refined-product demand weakness in 2024, EV/hybrid adoption slowing gasoline growth, and LNG/electric heavy-duty trucks reducing gasoil demand; NBS reports April/May 2026 refinery-run declines.
adjustment_behavior: Demand reduction is a real adjustment channel but hard to allocate by product without a balance sheet.
inference_vs_observed: Observed structural demand context; shock-specific magnitude inferred.
notes: Use as an explanation for why import cuts are not mechanically equal to shortages.
